title: Star Wars: Return of the Jedi: Death Star Battle
content:
{{Top|rwm|reb|italics=1}} {{VideoGame |image=[[File:B_StarWarsDeathStarBattle_front.jpg]] |title=''Star Wars: Return of the Jedi:
Death Star Battle'' |developer=[[Wickstead Design]]{{BookCite|author=DaddaRuleKonge |container=|title=The Atari 2600 Encyclopedia|link=https://archive.org/details/the-atari-2600-encyclopedia-book/mode/2up}} |publisher=[[Parker Brothers]] |release date=*[[October]] [[1983]] {{C|Atari 2600}}{{BookCite|container=Availability Update|title=The Video Game Update|number=Volume 2, Number 8|date=November 1983|link=https://segaretro.org/Computer_Entertainer}} *[[February]] [[1984]] {{C|Atari 5200}}{{BookCite|container=Availability Update|title=The Video Game Update|number=Volume 2, Number 11|date=February 1984|link=https://segaretro.org/Computer_Entertainer}} |closed= |upc= |engine= |genre=[[Wikipedia:Computer and video game genres#Action|Action]] |modes=One player |ratings= |platforms=[[Wikipedia:Atari 2600|Atari 2600]], [[Wikipedia:Atari 5200|Atari 5200]], [[Wikipedia:Atari 8-bit|Atari 8-bit]], [[Wikipedia:ZX Spectrum|ZX Spectrum]] |series= |basegame= |expansions= |director= |producer= |designer=[[Todd Marshall]], [[Ray Miller]]{{WebCite|url=http://www.2600connection.com/interviews/larry_gelberg/interview_larry_gelberg.html|text=Larry Gelberg Interview|work=2600 Connection|archivedate=20210616122433}} |programmer= |artist= |writer= |composer= |timeline= |prev= |next= }} '''''Star Wars: Return of the Jedi: Death Star Battle''''' is a game produced by [[Parker Brothers]] and released for the [[Wikipedia:Atari 2600|Atari 2600]] in [[1983]], and for the [[Wikipedia:Atari 5200|Atari 5200]], [[Wikipedia:Atari 8-bit|Atari 8-bit home computers]] and [[ZX Spectrum]] in [[1984]]. The object of the game is to pilot the [[Millennium Falcon/Legends|''Millennium Falcon'']] and destroy the second [[Death Star II/Legends|Death Star]], but you also have to defend yourself from [[TIE/IN interceptor/Legends|TIE interceptors]]. This game can be found online for free and unlimited plays. The cover art was designed by the renowned sci-fi artist [[John Berkey]], who designed some of the earliest poster art for [[Star Wars: Episode IV A New Hope|''Star Wars'': Episode IV ''A New Hope'']] in [[1976]].{{WebCite|url=https://johnberkeyart.com/star-wars-art|text=Star Wars Art – John Berkey Art|work=johnberkeyart.com|archivedate=20210228154650}} {{Game-stub}} ==Publisher's summary== ''Just when you thought the DEATH STAR had been reduced to dust—it's making a comeback! Piece by piece, the Empire's reconstructing the ultimate space station. Its completion would mean unthinkable power for the Imperial forces!'' ''The DEATH STAR must be destroyed... once and for all!'' ==Development== Early materials from Parker Brothers initially referred to the game as '''''Star Wars: Revenge of the Jedi: Game II''''', and then '''''Star Wars: Return of the Jedi: Game II'''''. The ultimately canceled ''[[Star Wars: Return of the Jedi: Ewok Adventure|Ewok Adventure]]'' game was ''Game I''. Some early materials feature placeholder artwork featuring the [[Battle of the Great Pit of Carkoon|battle at the sarlaac pit]] instead of the film's space battle.{{WebCite|url=http://www.atarimania.com/game-atari-2600-vcs-star-wars-return-of-the-jedi-death-star-battle_7928.html|text=Star Wars - Return of the Jedi - Death Star Battle|work=Atarimania.com|archivedate=20201126043445}} A version for [[Wikipedia:Intellivision|Intellivision]] was at one point slated for [[October]] [[1983]] but was never released. ==Media==
